description: Figure 9 presents a schematic diagram illustrating the precipitation behavior of κ-carbides and (Ti, V)C carbides in the M2 Fe–Mn–Al–C austenitic low-density steel during aging at 550 °C. The left panel depicts an austenite grain containing pre-existing intragranular (Ti, V)C carbides, where the color gradient indicates that carbon content is highest at the carbide center and decreases toward the edges. The right panel shows the microstructure after aging at 550 °C, where κ-carbides heterogeneously precipitate preferentially along the edges of the (Ti, V)C carbides rather than uniformly throughout the matrix. The legend identifies the blue hexagon as the austenite grain, white circles as (Ti, V)C carbides, and gray rectangles as κ-carbides. This schematic conveys that the carbon-enriched zones surrounding (Ti, V)C carbides provide favorable heterogeneous nucleation sites for κ-carbides, which is central to the Ti/V microalloying strategy for controlling precipitation morphology and thereby balancing strength and work-hardening capacity.
